questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Match the following
\(\begin{array}{l} \text {Column - I} & \text {Column - II} \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(A) If } \sum \mathrm{n}=210, \text{ then } \sum \mathrm{n}^2 \text{ is divisible by the} \\ \text{greatest prime number which is greater than} \end{array} & \text { (p) } 16 \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(B) Between } 4 \text{ and } 2916 \text{ is inserted odd number } (2 n+1) \\ \text{G.M's, then the } (\mathrm{n}+1) \text{ th G.M is divisible by the} \\ \text{greatest odd integer which is less than} \end{array} & \text { (q) } 10 \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(C) In a certain progression four consecutive terms are } 40, 30, 24, 20, \\ \text{ then the integral part of progression is more than} \end{array} & \text { (r) } 34 \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(D) } 1+\frac{4}{5}+\frac{7}{5^2}+\frac{10}{5^3}+\ldots .+\infty=\frac{a}{b}, \\ \text{ where H.C.F } (a, b)=1, \text{ then } (a-b) \text{ is less than} \end{array} & \text { (s) } 30 \\ & \text { (t) } 0 \\ \end{array}\)
questions.solution:
(A)
$\begin{aligned}
& \sum n=210 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}+1)=420 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=20 \\
& \therefore \sum \mathrm{n}^2=(10)(7)(41)
\end{aligned}$
(B) $4, G_1, G_2, \ldots \ldots G_{n+1}, G_{3 n}, G_{3-1}, 2916$
$\mathrm{G}_{\mathrm{n},-1}$ will be the middle mean of $(2 \mathrm{n}+1)$ odd means
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore G_{n+1}^2=4 \times 2916=4 \times 9 \times 324=4 \times 9 \times 4 \times 81 \\
& \therefore G_{n+1}=2 \times 3 \times 2 \times 9=108
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Greatest odd number by which $\mathrm{G}_{\mathrm{a}+1}$ is divisible is 27
(C) Terms are 40, 30, 24, 20
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \frac{1}{30}-\frac{1}{40}=\frac{1}{120} \\
& \frac{1}{24}-\frac{1}{30}=\frac{1}{24 \times 30}=\frac{1}{120} \\
& \frac{1}{20}-\frac{1}{24}=\frac{4}{20 \times 24}=\frac{1}{120}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \frac{1}{30} \cdot \frac{1}{24} \cdot \frac{1}{20}$ are in AB. witi common difference $\mathrm{d}=\frac{1}{120}$
$\therefore$ Next term is $\frac{1}{20}+\frac{1}{120}=\frac{7}{120}$
$\therefore$ Next term of given series is $\frac{120}{7}=17 \frac{1}{7}$
$\therefore$ Integral part $=17$
$\text { (D) } \begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{S}=1+\frac{4}{5}+\frac{7}{5^2}+\frac{10}{5^3}+\ldots \infty \\
& \frac{S}{5}=\frac{1}{5}+\frac{4}{5^2}+\frac{7}{5^3}+\ldots . . \infty \\
& \therefore S\left(1-\frac{1}{5}\right)=1+3\left(\frac{1}{5}+\frac{1}{5^2}+\ldots \infty\right) \\
& \frac{4}{5} S=1+3\left(\frac{\frac{1}{5}}{1-\frac{1}{5}}\right)=\frac{7}{4} \\
& S=\frac{35}{16} \cdot \mathrm{a}=35, \mathrm{~b}=16 \\
& \therefore \mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}=19
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Matrix Matching
questions.solution:
(A) $\frac{3 x+x+z / 2}{x+z-x}+\frac{3 x+x+z / 2}{x+z-z}=\frac{7}{2}\left(\frac{x}{z}+\frac{z}{x}\right)+1$
$\frac{x}{z}+\frac{z}{x} \geq 2$. Expression is $\geq 7+1=8$
(B)
$\begin{aligned}
& x_1+x_2+x_2=1 \\
& x_1+x_3=2 x_2 \Rightarrow x_2=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{27}-\frac{1}{9}+\frac{\alpha}{3}+\beta=0 \\
& \Rightarrow 1-3+9 \alpha+27 \beta=9 \alpha+27 \beta=2
\end{aligned}$
(C) Let the roots be $\alpha-3 \beta, \alpha-\beta, \alpha+\beta, \alpha+3 \beta$

Sum of roots, $4 \alpha=16$
$\Rightarrow \alpha=4$

Also. $\beta^2=1$ or $\frac{|5|}{9}, \beta^2=1$
$\Rightarrow \beta= \pm 1$

Roots are 1, 3, 5. 7. or 7, 5, 3, 1
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore d=2 \text { or } d=-2 \\
& \therefore 2 d^2+4=12
\end{aligned}$
(D)
$\begin{aligned}
& a_1+a_3+a_5+\ldots . .+a_{2 n-1}=a_4+a_6+a_8+\ldots . .+a_{2 n+2}=r^3\left(a_1+a_3+a_5+\ldots .+a_{2 n-1}\right) \\
& r^3=1 \\
& \Rightarrow r=1, \omega_1 \omega^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Two AP's having same number of terms equal to $k$. The ratio of the last term of the first progression to the first term of the second progression equals the ratio of the last term of the second progression to the first term of the first progression both of which are numerically equal to 4 . The ratio of the sum of $k$ terms of the first progression to the sum of $k$ terms of second progression is equal to 2 . Let $\alpha$ be the ratio of the common difference of the first and the second progressions. Let $\lambda$ be the ratio of their $k^{\text {th }}$ terms. Then:
Question: Which of the following options has the correct combination considering List-I and List-II?
questions.solution:
Let the first A.P. be $a_1, a_2, a_3, \ldots . ., a_k$
and the second A.P. be $b_1, b_2, b_3, \ldots \ldots, b_k$
Given
$$
\frac{a_k}{b_1}=\frac{b_k}{a_1}=4
$$
$$
\Rightarrow \begin{array}{lll}
a_k=4 b_1 & \Rightarrow & a_1+(k-1) d_1=4 b_1 ...(1) \\
b_k=4 a_1 & \Rightarrow & b_1+(k-1) d_2=4 a_1 ... (2)
\end{array}
$$

Given $\quad \frac{S_k}{S_k^{\prime}}=2 \Rightarrow \frac{2 a_1+(k-1) d_1}{2 b_1+(k-1) d_2}=2$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{a_1+\overbrace{a_1+(k-1) d_1}^{b_1+(k-1) d_2}}{b_1}=2 \\
& \frac{a_1+4 b_1}{b_1+4 a_1}=2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a_1+4 b_1=2 b_1+8 a_1
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad \frac{a_1}{b_1}=\frac{2}{7} ... (3)
$$

From eqns. (1) and (2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (k-1) d_1=4 b_1-a_1 \\
& (k-1) d_2=4 a_1-b_1
\end{aligned}
$$

On dividing, we get
$$
\frac{d_1}{d_2}=\frac{4 b_1-a_1}{4 a_1-b_1}=\frac{4-(2 / 7)}{4(2 / 7)-1}=26=\alpha
$$

Also
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\text { Also } & \lambda=\frac{a_k}{b_k}=\frac{b_1}{a_1}=\frac{7}{2} \\
\therefore & \alpha+2 \lambda=26+7=33
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Two AP's having same number of terms equal to $k$. The ratio of the last term of the first progression to the first term of the second progression equals the ratio of the last term of the second progression to the first term of the first progression both of which are numerically equal to 4 . The ratio of the sum of $k$ terms of the first progression to the sum of $k$ terms of second progression is equal to 2 . Let $\alpha$ be the ratio of the common difference of the first and the second progressions. Let $\lambda$ be the ratio of their $k^{\text {th }}$ terms. Then:
Question: Which of the following options has the incorrect combination considering List-I and List-II?
questions.solution:
Let the first A.P. be $a_1, a_2, a_3, \ldots . ., a_k$
and the second A.P. be $b_1, b_2, b_3, \ldots \ldots, b_k$
Given
$$
\frac{a_k}{b_1}=\frac{b_k}{a_1}=4
$$
$$
\Rightarrow \begin{array}{lll}
a_k=4 b_1 & \Rightarrow & a_1+(k-1) d_1=4 b_1 ...(1) \\
b_k=4 a_1 & \Rightarrow & b_1+(k-1) d_2=4 a_1 ... (2)
\end{array}
$$

Given $\quad \frac{S_k}{S_k^{\prime}}=2 \Rightarrow \frac{2 a_1+(k-1) d_1}{2 b_1+(k-1) d_2}=2$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{a_1+\overbrace{a_1+(k-1) d_1}^{b_1+(k-1) d_2}}{b_1}=2 \\
& \frac{a_1+4 b_1}{b_1+4 a_1}=2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a_1+4 b_1=2 b_1+8 a_1
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad \frac{a_1}{b_1}=\frac{2}{7} ... (3)
$$

From eqns. (1) and (2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (k-1) d_1=4 b_1-a_1 \\
& (k-1) d_2=4 a_1-b_1
\end{aligned}
$$

On dividing, we get
$$
\frac{d_1}{d_2}=\frac{4 b_1-a_1}{4 a_1-b_1}=\frac{4-(2 / 7)}{4(2 / 7)-1}=26=\alpha
$$

Also
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\text { Also } & \lambda=\frac{a_k}{b_k}=\frac{b_1}{a_1}=\frac{7}{2} \\
\therefore & \alpha+2 \lambda=26+7=33
\end{array}
$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $t_1, t_2, t_3, \ldots \ldots, t_9$ are positive numbers such that $t_1 \cdot t_2 \cdot \ldots . . \cdot t_9=3^7$ and $m=t_1+t_2+\ldots \ldots+t_7+3 t_8+3 t_9$ and $n=\frac{1}{t_1}+\frac{1}{t_2}+\ldots \ldots+\frac{1}{t_7}+\frac{1}{3 t_8}+\frac{1}{3 t_9}$, then:
questions.solution:
(P) $m \geq 9 \cdot\left(3^9\right)^{1 / 9} \quad \Rightarrow \quad m \geq 27$
(Q) $\frac{n}{9} \geq \frac{1}{3} \quad \Rightarrow \quad n \geq 3$
(R) $\sum_{i=1}^9 t_i=3 \times 7+2=23$
(S) $\quad \sum_{i=1}^9 t_i=3 \times 7+2=23$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: b and c are arithmetic means between a and $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{a}\gt\mathrm{d}\gt0)$ and h and k are the geometric mean between a and $d$ then
options: bc is always greater than hk
bc is always less than hk
bc may be equal to hk
questions.solution: Let $a-d=3 t$; then $b c=(d+z t)(d+t)=d^2+3 t d+2 t^2$ Also $\frac{a}{d}=1+\frac{3 t}{d}$, so $h k=d^2\left[1+\frac{3 t}{d}\right]=d^2 t+3 t d \lt b c$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{bc}\gt\mathrm{hk}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a_1, a_2, a_3, \ldots \ldots$, , are in arithmetic progression, then $S=a_1^2-a_2^2+a_3^2-a_4^2+\ldots \ldots-a_{2 k}^2$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$
S=-d\left(a_1+a_2+\ldots \ldots+a_{2 k}\right) \text {, where } d \text { is common difference }
$$
$$
S=-d \cdot \frac{2 k}{2} \cdot\left(a_1+a_{2 k}\right)=-k d\left(a_1+a_{2 k}\right)........(1)
$$

Now,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& a_2-a_1=d \\
& a_3-a_2=d
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\frac{a_{2 k}-a_{2 k-1}=d}{a_{2 k}-a_1=(2 k-1) d}
$$

Add $\frac{a_{2 k}-a_{2 k-1}=d}{a_{2 k}-a_1=(2 k-1) d}........(2)$
From eqn. (1) and eqn. (2)
$$
S=\frac{k}{2 k-1}\left(a_1^2-a_{2 k}^2\right)
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Three friends whose ages form a G.P. divide a certain sum of money in proportion to their ages. If they do that three years later, when the youngest is half the age of the oldest, then he will receive Rs. 150/- more than he gets now, and the middle friend will get Rs. 15 more than now, then the age of the youngest friend is
questions.solution:
Let the age of friends be $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{ar}, \mathrm{ar}^2, \mathrm{r}\gt1$ and money distributed is $\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{xr}, \mathrm{xr}^2$ respectively.
$\Rightarrow\left(1+r+r^2\right) x=\left(a\left(1+r+r^2\right)+a\right) t$
Also after 3 years $a r^2+3=2(a+3)$
$\Rightarrow a\left(r^2-2\right)=3$
$x+105=(a+3) t$ and $x \mathrm{r}+15=(\mathrm{ar}+3) \mathrm{t}$ (qiven)
$\Rightarrow(1+r) x+120=(a(1+r)+6) t \Rightarrow 2(a+3) t=r^2 x-120=2 x+210$
$\Rightarrow\left(r^2-2\right) x=330$
Using $\left(r^2-2\right) x=330, a\left(r^2-2\right)=3 \Rightarrow x=110 a$
$\Rightarrow 110 a+105=(a+3) t \Rightarrow 5(22 a+21)=(a+3) t$
and 110ar $+15=(a r+3) t \Rightarrow 5(22 a r+3)=(a r+3) t$
and 110ar $+15=(a r+3) t \Rightarrow 5(22 a r+3)=(a r+3) t$
$\Rightarrow \frac{22 a+21}{22 a r+3}=\frac{a+3}{a r+3} \Rightarrow 5 a r=7 a+6$
Where 5ar $=7 \mathrm{a}+2\left(\mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{r}^2-2\right)\right)\left\{\right.$ Using $\left.\mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{r}^2-2\right)=3\right\}$
$\Rightarrow 2 r^2-5 r+3=0 \Rightarrow r=\frac{3}{2}$. Hence $a=12$ years
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $ \lt a_n\gt$ and $ \lt b_n\gt$ be the arithmetic sequences each with common difference 2 such that $a_1 \lt b_1$ and let $c_n=\sum_{k=1}^n a_k, d_n=\sum_{k=1}^n b_k$. Suppose that the points $A_n\left(a_n, c_n\right), B_n\left(b_n, d_n\right)$ are all lies on the parabola $C: y=p x^2+q x+r$ where $p, q, r$ are the constants.Question: The value of $p$ equals
questions.solution:
Given $c_n=a_1+a_2+a_3+\ldots . .+a_n$
where $a_1, a_2, \ldots . ., a_n$ are in A.P. with $d=2$
and $d_n=b_1+b_2+b_3+\ldots \ldots+b_n$
where $b_1, b_2, b_3, \ldots b_n$ are in A.P. with $d=2$
Also $\left(a_n, c_n\right)$ lies on $y=p x^2+q x+r$
Now $c_n=p a_n^2+q a_n+r$    ....(1)
$c_{n-1}=p a_{n-1}^2+q a_{n-1}+r$    ....(2)
$\therefore$ From (1) and (2), we get
$c_n-c_{n-1}=p\left(a_n^2-a_{n-1}^2\right)+q\left(a_n-a_{n-1}\right)$
$\Rightarrow a_n=p\left(a_n+a_{n-1}\right)\left(a_n-a_{n-1}\right)+q\left(a_n-a_{n-1}\right)$
$a_n=\left(a_n-a_{n-1}\right)\left[p\left(a_n+a_{n-1}\right)+q\right]$    ....(3)
$\left(a_n-a_{n-1}=d\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $S_r, r \in N$, denote the sum of an infinite GP whose first term is $2 r-1$ and common ratio $\frac{1}{2 r}$. Let $V_n=\sum_{r=1}^n S_r$ and $Q_n=\sum_{r=1}^n \frac{1}{S_r S_{r+1}}$Question: $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{V_n}{n^2}$ equals
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & V_n=\sum_{r=1}^n S_r=2 \sum_{r=1}^n r=n(n+1) \\ & \therefore \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{V_n}{n^2}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{n+1}{n}=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of all polynomial whose coefficients are all $\pm 1$, that have only real roots are
questions.solution:
If a polynomial $a_0 x^n+a_1 x^{n-1}+\ldots . .+a_n$ is such a polynomial, We can assume $a_0=1$, let $r_1, r_2, \ldots . ., r_n$ are its roots

$\Rightarrow r_1^2+r_2^2+\ldots . .+r_n^2=a_1^2-2 a_2, r_1^2 r_2^2 \ldots . . r_n^2=a_n^2$

$\Rightarrow A M \geq G M \Rightarrow \frac{a_1^2-2 a_2}{n} \geq\left(a_n\right)^{2 / n}$

$\because a_1, a_2= \pm 1$, we must have $a_2=-1$, and $\mathrm{n} \leq 3$
$\Rightarrow$ Polynomials are
$\pm(x-1), \pm(x+1), \pm\left(x^2+x-1\right), \pm\left(x^2-x-1\right), \pm\left(x^3+x^2-x-1\right), \pm\left(x^3-x^2-x+1\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a_1, a_2, a_3 \ldots$. are in A.P. and $b_k=a_k+a_{k+1}+\ldots .+a_{k+n-1}(k=1,2,3 \ldots$.$) , then b_1+b_2+\ldots . .+b_n$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { We have } b_k=\frac{n}{2}\left(a_k+a_{n+k-1}\right)=\frac{n}{2}\left[a_1+(k-1) d+a_1+(n+k-2) d\right] \\ & =\frac{n}{2}\left[a_n+a_1+2(k-1) d\right] \text { (d is the common difference) } \\ & \Rightarrow \sum_{k=1}^n b_k=\frac{n}{2}\left[n a_n+n a_1+2 d \sum_{k=1}^n(k-1)\right]=\frac{n}{2}\left[n a_n+n a_1+d(n-1) n\right] \\ & =\frac{n^2}{2}\left[a_n+a_1+(n-1) d\right]=\frac{n^2}{2}\left[a_n+a_n\right]=n^2 a_n\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The sequence $a_n$ is defined by $a_1=\frac{1}{2}, a_{n+1}=a_n^2+a_n$. Also, $s=\frac{1}{a_1+1}+\frac{1}{a_2+1}+\ldots . .+\frac{1}{a_{100}+1}$ then $[\mathrm{S}]$ (where [.] denotes the greatest integer function) is
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{a_{n+1}}=\frac{1}{a_n\left(a_n+1\right)}=\frac{1}{a_n}-\frac{1}{a_n+1}$
$\Rightarrow S=\frac{1}{a_1}-\frac{1}{a_2}+\ldots . .+\frac{1}{a_{100}}-\frac{1}{a_{101}}$
$=\frac{1}{a_1}-\frac{1}{a_{101}}=2-\frac{1}{a_{101}}$
Since, $a_{101}\gt1 \Rightarrow[S]=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a sequence $f(x), n \in I^{+} \cup\{0\}$ defined by $f(0)=0, f(n+1)=\sqrt{6+f(n)}$ then $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} f(n)$ is
questions.solution:
$f(0) \lt f(1)$ now if suppose
$\Rightarrow f(n-1) \lt f(n)$
$\Rightarrow \sqrt{f(n-1)+6} \lt \sqrt{f(n)+6}$
$\Rightarrow f(n) \lt f(n+1)$
Also, $f(0) \lt 3$, let $f(n) \lt 3$
$\Rightarrow \sqrt{f(n)+6} \lt \sqrt{3+6}$
$\Rightarrow f(n+1) \lt 3$
So, $f(n)$ is bounded above 3
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $T_r=\frac{r}{r^4+4}$ and $S_n=\sum_{r=1}^n t_r$, then the value of $37 S_5-\frac{7}{26}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & T_r=\frac{r}{r^4+4}=\frac{r}{\left(r^2+2\right)^2-4 r^2}=\frac{r}{\left(r^2+2 r+2\right)\left(r^2-2 r+2\right)} \\ & =\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{1}{r^2-2 r+2}-\frac{1}{r^2+2 r+2}\right]=\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{1}{(r-1)^2+1}-\frac{1}{(r+1)^2+1}\right] \\ & \Rightarrow S_n=\frac{1}{4}\left[1+\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{n^2+1}-\frac{1}{(n+1)^2+1}\right] \\ & \Rightarrow S_5=\frac{1}{4}\left[1+\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{26}-\frac{1}{37}\right]=\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{38 \times 37-26 \times 1}{26 \times 37}\right]=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1380}{26 \times 37}=\frac{345}{26 \times 37} \\ & \Rightarrow 37 S_5-\frac{7}{26}=\frac{338}{26}=13\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x^2+9 y^2+25 z^2=x y z\left(\frac{15}{x}+\frac{5}{y}+\frac{3}{z}\right)$, the $x, y, z$ are in
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x^2+9 y^2+25 z^2=15 y z+5 z x+3 x y \\ & (x)^2+(3 y)^2+(5 z)^2-(x)(3 y)-(3 y)(5 z)-(x)(5 z)=0 \\ & \frac{1}{2}\left[2(x)^2+2(3 y)^2+2(5 z)^2-2(x)(3 y)-2(3 y)(5 z)-2(x)(5 z)\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{2}\left[(x-3 y)^2+(3 y-5 z)^2+(x-5 z)^2\right]=0 \\ & x-3 y=0,3 y-5 z=0, x-5 z=0 \\ & \frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{3 y} \text { and } \frac{5}{3 y}=\frac{1}{z} \text { and } \frac{1}{5 z}=\frac{1}{x} \\ & \frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{z}=\frac{1}{3 y}+\frac{5}{3 y}=\frac{2}{y}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \frac{1}{x}, \frac{1}{y}, \frac{1}{z}$ are in A.P. and $x, y, z$ are in H.P.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a, b, c \in R^{+}$then the minimum value of $a\left(b^2+c^2\right)+b\left(c^2+a^2\right)+c\left(a^2+b^2\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\frac{a b^2+a c^2+b c^2+b a^2+c a^2+c b^2}{6} \geq\left(a^6 b^6 c^6\right)^{1 / 6}$
So $a\left(b^2+c^2\right)+b\left(c^2+a^2\right)+c\left(a^2+b^2\right) \geq 6 a b c$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $S$ denote the sum to infinity and $S_n$ the sum of $n$ terms of the series $1+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{9}+\frac{1}{27}+\ldots$ such that $S-S_n \lt \frac{1}{300}$, then the least value of $n$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & S-S_n \lt \frac{1}{300} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{1-\frac{1}{3}}-\frac{1-\frac{1}{3^n}}{1-\frac{1}{3}} \lt \frac{1}{300} \Rightarrow \frac{3}{2}\left[1-1+\frac{1}{3^n}\right] \lt \frac{1}{300} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3^n} \lt \frac{1}{300} \times \frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3^n} \lt \frac{1}{450} \Rightarrow 3^n\gt450 \\ & \therefore \text { Least value of } n=6\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The first two terms of a geometric progression add up to 12 . The sum of third and fourth terms is
48. If the terms are alternately positive and negative, then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& a+a r=12 ....(1)\\
& a r^2+a r^3=48 ....(2)\\
& \therefore r^2=4 \Rightarrow r=-2, \because r \neq-1,2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x, y, z$ are in A.P. and $\tan ^{-1} x, \tan ^{-1} y$ and $\tan ^{-1} z$ are also in A.P., then:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 2 \tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} z \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} \frac{2 y}{1-y^2}=\tan ^{-1} \frac{x+z}{1-x z} \\ & y^2=x z \\ & \text { also } 2 y=x+z \\ & \therefore x=z=y \quad(x, y, z \text { are in A.P. and G.P. })\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\frac{a_2 a_3}{a_1 a_4}=\frac{a_2+a_3}{a_1+a_4}=3\left(\frac{a_2-a_3}{a_1-a_4}\right)$, then $a_1, a_2, a_3, a_4$ are in
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{a_4}-\frac{1}{a_3}=\frac{1}{a_2}-\frac{1}{a_1}$  ...(i)
$3\left(\frac{1}{a_3}-\frac{1}{a_2}\right)=\frac{1}{a_4}-\frac{1}{a_1}$   ...(ii)
$\therefore$ From (1) and (2), we get $\frac{1}{a_2}-\frac{1}{a_1}=\frac{1}{a_3}-\frac{1}{a_2}=\frac{1}{a_4}-\frac{1}{a_3}$
$\therefore \mathrm{a}_1, \mathrm{a}_2, \mathrm{a}_3, \mathrm{a}_4$ are in H.P.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The $\mathrm{m}^{\text {th }}$ term of an arithmetic progression is x and the $\mathrm{n}^{\text {th }}$ term is $y$. Then the sum of the first $(m+n)$ terms is
questions.solution:
Let first term is a then $x=a+(m-1) d, y=a+(n-1) d$ and $s=\frac{m+n}{2}[2 a+(m+n-1) d]$ $(x+y)=2 a+(m+n-2) d$ and $(x-y)=(m-n) d$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow d=\left(\frac{x-y}{m-n}\right) \Rightarrow(x+y)=2 a+(m+n-1) d-\left(\frac{x+y}{m-n}\right) \\ & S=\left(\frac{m+n}{2}\right)\left[(x+y)+\frac{x-y}{m-n}\right]\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: In $a \triangle A B C, A, B, C$ are in $A P$ and $a, b, c$ are in GP then value of $a^3+b^3+c^3-a^2 b-b^2 c-c^2 a$ is
questions.solution:
$\triangle A B C$ is equilateral
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{b}=\mathrm{c}$
